title: implement-plan: stateful installs — interactive selection, audit log, re-run UX

comments.author: Joe Black
comments.text: State file location updated: state.json → ~/Library/Application Support/aip/state.json (macOS) / ~/.local/share/aip/state.json (Linux). Run logs → ~/Library/Logs/aip/. Use env-paths npm package for cross-platform path resolution. Does NOT live in the repo — user-specific state.

title: implement-plan: agents: frontmatter does not drive multi-agent mcp-config installs
Canonicalize npx skills as the cross-agent skill manager
description:
The 'npx skills' CLI (vercel-labs/skills) is already the skill manager on this machine. ~/.agents/.skill-lock.json is its lock file. This task is to properly document and standardize it as the one true way to install skills going forward.

## What exists today

- ~/.agents/skills/ = shared pool (25+ skills)
- ~/.claude/skills, ~/.gemini/skills, ~/.cursor/skills, ~/.opencode/skills → symlinks to shared pool
- ~/.codex/skills/ = real dir with Codex-specific skills (pdf, self-learning) + shared skills installed separately
- ~/.agents/.skill-lock.json = npx skills lock file (tracks source, hash, installedAt per skill)
- ~/.agents/AGENTS.md = canonical instruction file; ~/.codex/AGENTS.md and ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md symlink to it

## Work items

1. Install 'skills' globally: npm i -g skills
2. Audit which agents are actively used (Warp? Amp? GitHub Copilot? Kimi CLI?) and configure agent targets
3. Re-run installs for all shared skills with correct agent targets so skill-lock.json reflects reality
4. Document the canonical install workflow in this repo's README or docs/
5. Decide and document: should ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md also become a symlink to ~/.agents/AGENTS.md for full consistency?
6. Add health-check script to ~/.agents/bin/check-skills that validates all symlinks and prints status per agent
7. Update cross-agent audit doc at ~/.claude/handoffs/cross-agent-skills-system.md

## Agent install command (shared skills)

npx skills add <repo> -a claude-code -a cursor -a gemini-cli -a opencode -a codex

## Notes

- anthropics/skills@pdf is the right PDF skill for Claude/Cursor/Gemini (89K installs)
- ~/.codex/skills/pdf and self-learning are Codex-native, stay Codex-only
- Antigravity skills (~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/) are a separate managed pool, do not merge
notes: Audit doc at ~/.claude/handoffs/cross-agent-skills-system.md — NOTE: partially stale as of 2026-04-30. Phase 1 (Cursor + OpenCode symlinks) is done, code-review-excellence/gh-address-comments/gh-fix-ci migrated to shared pool, ~/.agents/AGENTS.md created, ~/.codex/AGENTS.md and ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md now symlink to it. Still accurate for: Codex skills dir not yet symlinked, pdf/self-learning staying Codex-only, npx skills canonicalization pending.

title: Wire claude-mem worker auth to respect active claude account (primary/vertex/glm switcher)
notes: Root cause: ~/.claude-mem/.env now has CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN for primary account hardcoded. Worker survives launchd restarts. Remaining issue: when user switches to vertex/glm account, worker still uses primary token — needs dynamic account detection or launchd env injection per active account. Config: ~/.config/claude/accounts/{primary,secondary,vertex}.env. Worker port 37777.

title: communication: always disambiguate ambiguous file/path references (e.g. 'settings.json' → specify full path or context like 'Claude Code settings (~/.claude/settings.json)' vs 'project settings.json')
seed-global-memory.ts: one-time script to populate ~/.claude/MEMORY.md from claude-mem observations (Phase 3 — no script written yet)
File upstream: writing-plans injects agentic-worker header for docs-only plans
description:
The writing-plans superpowers skill unconditionally adds a 'for agentic workers' header to every plan that triggers superpowers:executing-plans or subagent-driven-development — even when the plan contains zero source code.

This cascades into: post-task code review (no code), lesson emission (no bugs), simplify invocations (no code to simplify).

Fix needed in writing-plans skill: detect docs-only plans (all file actions are .md / config / CLI commands, no .ts/.py/.js/.sh source files) and emit a lightweight header variant that says 'execute directly in-session, skip code review pipeline.'

Workaround: rule added to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md 2026-04-23.

---

ADDITIONAL: bd init has no --no-claude flag to suppress CLAUDE.md injection. Workaround is to run 'bd setup claude --remove' immediately after 'bd init'. Feature request: add bd init --no-claude (or respect a global config key like claude.inject=false) to skip CLAUDE.md injection when a global beads integration is already configured.

title: Generate timeline-report once session summaries accumulate
description: Session summarization was fixed 2026-04-23. Revisit after 5+ sessions have accumulated. Use the claude-mem:timeline-report skill to generate a narrative summary of recent project work.

title: Build joe-preferences claude-mem corpus
description:
Run in a Claude session with claude-mem MCP available:

  build_corpus name="joe-preferences"     description="Cross-project preferences, patterns, stack choices, and working style"     limit=500

Then:
  prime_corpus name="joe-preferences"

Verify with: list_corpora

---

BLOCKED: Corpus tools (build_corpus, prime_corpus, list_corpora) are described in the
knowledge-agent skill but NOT implemented in claude-mem worker-service v12.2.0 or v12.3.9.
The /api/corpus route returns 404. Only 'teicorpus' stub exists in worker-service.cjs.

Tried: npx claude-mem update → reinstalls same plugin version (cache unchanged).

NEXT STEP: Check claude-mem changelog/roadmap for corpus implementation. May need to
wait for a future release or file a feature request upstream (add to ai-6vl).
